source.sourcePath: raw/sources/DHF (인허가)/09_HnVUE/92_인증 - FDA/참고샘플 - Aspenview/AspenView Cybersecurity Risk Management - SBOM End of Support.pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: AspenView internal cybersecurity risk management SBOM section listing software component support lifecycle, component categories, and security assessment notes.
keyPoints: The document identifies an SBOM and support lifecycle section with module names, versions, and level of support.
Listed modules are categorized as commercial, open-source, and off-the-shelf software components.
All listed software components, whether commercial or open-source, are considered off-the-shelf because they are pre-developed and ready for use.
The security assessment notes monitoring of updates, patches, and security vulnerabilities for selected components.

entities.name: Microsoft
entities.evidence: Regular updates are provided by Microsoft, which often include security patches.

entities.name: Apache Software Foundation
entities.evidence: log4net XML External Entity(XXE) vulnerability, which has been patched by Apache Software Foundation

entities.name: GitHub
entities.evidence: any security vulnerabilities might be discussed in the project's official repositories on GitHub

concepts.name: SBOM and Support Lifecycle
concepts.pageTarget: Software Bill of Materials Support Lifecycle

concepts.name: DICOM standards
concepts.evidence: DICOM standards ensure that the patients’ sensitive data are encrypted

evidence.claim: The document initiate date is 2024.06.30 and the revision is Rev 2.
The document is marked HAEA Internal Use Only.
The SBOM lists module names, versions, and level of support.
evidence.evidenceText: Module Name Version Level of Support
evidence.pageTarget: Software Bill of Materials Support Lifecycle

evidence.claim: The SBOM component categorization section categorizes listed modules into commercial, open-source, and off-the-shelf types.
evidence.evidenceText: This section will categorize the listed modules into commercial, open-source, and off-the-shelf types.

evidence.claim: Commercial software components include Microsoft Visual Studio 2022 and Intel Integrated Performance Primitives.
evidence.evidenceText: 1. Microsoft Visual Studio, 2022 - Commercial 2. Intel(R) Integrated Performance Primitives, 8.1.1 - Commercial

evidence.claim: Open-source software components include BitMiracle.LibTiff.NET, ClosedXML, fo-dicom, log4net, Newtonsoft.Json, opencv, PdfSharp, protobuf-net, SixLabors.ImageSharp, and StructureMap.
All listed commercial and open-source software components are considered off-the-shelf.
evidence.evidenceText: Generally, all listed software components, whether commercial or open-source, are considered off-the-shelf as they are pre-developed and ready for use

evidence.claim: AspenView will regularly monitor BitMiracle LibTiff.NET and LibJpeg.NetCore repositories for patches that mention security fixes.
evidence.evidenceText: AspenView will regularly monitor for any patches that mention security fixes.

evidence.claim: AspenView uses logging methods only to record user activity for security monitoring and the logging is not customer facing.
evidence.evidenceText: AspenView uses logging methods within the software only to record user activity for security monitoring purposes, and these loggings are not customer facing

evidence.claim: The document states that DICOM standards ensure patient sensitive data are encrypted.
evidence.evidenceText: DICOM standards ensure that the patients’ sensitive data are encrypted

reviewFlags.reason: Authority metadata/source path indicates FDA, but the source text does not contain an FDA authority statement; authority or jurisdiction is therefore left empty.
Source text excerpt begins at AspenView Document 33/37 and may not include earlier title or regulatory metadata pages.
Potential conflicting version evidence for Newtonsoft.Json: module list shows 13.0.3, while open-source categorization shows 13.0.0.0.
Product metadata indicates HnVUE, but the provided source text identifies AspenView and does not mention HnVUE.
